description: To lead another along a path, showing the way through terrain physical or conceptual. 'Kulo' implies knowledge of the territory and responsibility for the guided one's safe passage. The guide goes along rather than pointing from a distance; presence is part of the work.
sound_symbolism: The word is compact and purposeful. 'Ku' begins with velar certainty—the guide knows where to go. The deep 'u' holds steady direction. Then 'lo' flows forward with liquid ease, the path unfolding, movement continuing.
grammatical_notes: As verb: 'mia thia kulo.' (I guide you.) With a path: 'mia thue thepalu thia kulo.' (I guide you through the garden.) As noun, names the one who guides. Related to but distinct from 'thumela' (teach); guiding is about paths, teaching about knowledge.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The spiritual friend (kalyana-mitta) guides not by commanding but by walking alongside, showing through example.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Guides carry ancestral knowledge of terrain—safe passages, seasonal dangers, hidden resources. Guidance is transmitted presence.
